title: GitHub Pages
description: GitHub Pages turns repositories into static websites — zero-config hosting with a built-in build pipeline and custom domain support.

sections.title: Setup & Deployment
sections.description:
Pages can be deployed from a branch (main or gh-pages) or a /docs folder. Organization/user sites (org.github.io) use the main branch of a repo named org.github.io. Project sites live at org.github.io/repo. Deploy via GitHub Actions for full control.

# Deploy branch setup
git checkout --orphan gh-pages
git rm -rf
echo "Hello World" > index.html
git add . && git commit -m "pages"
git push origin gh-pages

sections.title: Jekyll Integration
sections.description:
Pages natively supports Jekyll static site generation. Add a _config.yml, layouts in _layouts/, and posts in _posts/. Use front matter for metadata. .nojekyll file disables the build step for plain HTML/CSS/JS sites.

_config.yml
theme: jekyll-theme-cayman
# Front matter example
---
layout: post
title: "My Post"
---

sections.title: Custom Domains
sections.description:
Add a CNAME file with your domain in the repo root. Configure DNS: A records for apex domains (example.com → 185.199.108.153 and 3 others), CNAME for subdomains (www → username.github.io). GitHub provisions HTTPS certificates automatically via Let's Encrypt.

# CNAME file (repo root)
example.com
# DNS A records (apex)
185.199.108.153
185.199.109.153
185.199.110.153
185.199.111.153

sections.title: Actions Deployment
sections.description:
Use peaceiris/actions-gh-pages or JamesIves/github-pages-deploy-action for custom builds. The action builds your static site (npm, Hugo, Next.js) and pushes to gh-pages. Set publish_dir to the build output directory.

# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
- uses: actions/configure-pages@v4
- uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.description:
Build failures appear in repo Settings > Pages. Common issues: missing _config.yml for Jekyll, unsupported plugins, large file limits (1 GB), and custom domain propagation delays (up to 24h for HTTPS). Use the build log to debug.

# Check deployment status
https://api.github.com/repos/user/repo/pages
# Force HTTPS in settings
github.com/user/repo/settings/pages

details: GitHub Pages is powered by Fastly CDN with points of presence globally. Builds run on GitHub's build servers with a 10-minute timeout and 1 GB artifact limit. Supported static site generators include Jekyll (native), Hugo, Next.js, Gatsby, and any npm/build-tool-based generator via Actions. HTTPS is automatic for *.github.io and custom domains via Let's Encrypt — provisioning takes up to 16 hours for new custom domains. The CNAME file in the repo root is read during build and configures the CDN routing. For organization pages, the repo must be named organization.github.io. GitHub stores the latest build artifact; you can also configure Actions to deploy from any branch using the actions/deploy-pages action.
cheatSheet:
Core Commands

  • CNAME file with example.com — configure custom domain.
  • echo "Hello" > index.html && git push — simplest deploy.
  • .nojekyll — disable Jekyll for static sites.
  • peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3 — deploy via Actions.

Staff+ Patterns

  • Use Actions-based deployment (not the built-in branch deployer) for build control — run tests, build, then deploy. The built-in deployer has a 10-min timeout and limited builder support.
  • Add a robots.txt to prevent staging/non-production Pages from being indexed by search engines.

Watch Outs

  • HTTPS provisioning for new custom domains can take up to 16 hours. Plan domain changes ahead of launches.
  • Pages has a 1 GB artifact limit and 10-minute build timeout. Large sites need external CI/CD.

Interview Angle

Google L6 asks about CDN architecture: how does GitHub serve Pages from Fastly? Discuss edge caching, cache invalidation on deploy, and how CNAME routes traffic. Static site delivery is a simple case of a content delivery network design problem.
